incidents.headline: Meta executes 8,000-person AI-pivot restructuring globally
incidents.summary: On 20 May 2026, Meta Platforms began notifying approximately 8,000 employees worldwide — roughly 10% of its ~78,000-person workforce — that they were being laid off, starting with Singapore-based staff at 4 a.m. local time and cascading to UK and US workers. The restructuring, framed in an internal memo by Chief People Officer Janelle Gale, simultaneously cancels plans to hire 6,000 people and redirects ~7,000 existing staff into new AI-focused teams including Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator. The overhaul — the largest companywide cut since Zuckerberg's 2022-23 'Year of Efficiency' — is driven by a need to fund $125-145 billion in 2026 AI capital expenditure, with Bank of America projecting $7-8 billion in annualised savings.

secondary_mappings.why: 8,000 permanent job eliminations and simultaneous 7,000-person role reallocation constitute a direct, large-scale workforce harm requiring workforce-planning controls.
Zuckerberg's 'success isn't a given' memo and public disclosure of the restructuring create significant reputational exposure with employees, advertisers, and regulators globally.
Layoffs executed simultaneously across Singapore, UK, US and other jurisdictions trigger varying WARN-Act, TUPE, and local notice-period obligations that must be managed per country.

The entire restructuring is predicated on a strategic bet that AI agents will displace internal human roles; if the AI build-out underperforms, the organisational architecture designed around it becomes a liability.

incidents.severity_rationale: Affects ~10% of global workforce of a $1T+ market-cap company in a single execution window, with a further ~20% of total headcount touched when combined role changes are included; BoA projects $7-8B in annualised savings, signalling material strategic irreversibility.
incidents.financial_impact_disclosed: $7-8 billion annualised savings (Bank of America projection); $125-145 billion 2026 capex context

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure major structural transformations — including the reallocation of ~15,000 effective headcount positions (8,000 cuts + 7,000 redirections) and cancellation of 6,000 open requisitions — are governed by a documented strategic rationale, board-approved capital-allocation plan, and measurable outcome thresholds.

Ensure the AI-pivot innovation strategy underpinning the Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator teams is tied to explicit capability milestones and risk-adjusted returns that justify the $125-145B 2026 capex commitment.

Ensure workforce planning decisions, including the 8,000-person reduction executed from 20 May 2026 across Singapore, UK, and US jurisdictions, are preceded by a legally disaggregated impact assessment that accounts for each jurisdiction's notification and severance obligations.

Ensure that the redeployment of ~7,000 employees into new AI-focused teams, including Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator, is governed by documented reskilling, role-fit assessment, and retention obligations to prevent constructive dismissal exposure.

Ensure that external and internal communications about the 20 May 2026 restructuring — including the Janelle Gale memo framing cuts as efficiency-driven — are consistent with public regulatory disclosures and do not create a reputational gap between internal messaging and disclosed financial rationale.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: The board-level strategic review must formally document that the 8
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's board audit or finance committee must produce a Capital Allocation Decision Record (CADR) that explicitly documents the causal chain between the 8,000-person workforce reduction announced 20 May 2026 and the $125–145 billion 2026 AI capex commitment, including the quantum of annualised cost relief expected to fund that capex envelope. The CADR must be reviewed and signed by the CFO and General Counsel, and filed in the board minute register no later than the business day preceding Meta's next 10-Q submission deadline; the filing date must be recorded in the disclosure controls checklist maintained under SOX Section 302. If the CADR is not filed before the 10-Q is submitted, the disclosure controls officer must flag a remediation item to the audit committee within 24 hours of the missed deadline.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires board documentation of capex-versus-restructuring linkage; this adaptation binds that obligation to Meta's specific $125–145B 2026 figure, the 20 May 2026 notification date, the next 10-Q hard deadline, and named sign-off roles, converting an abstract governance requirement into a time-bound filing obligation with an escalation trigger.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Capital governance must stress-test the $125-145B 2026 AI
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's capital governance function must commission a stress-test scenario — to be completed within 60 days of 20 May 2026 — modelling the financial and operational consequences of both the Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator pods failing to reach defined productivity targets by Q2 2027 (four quarters post-launch), and must quantify the minimum incremental capex relief and headcount actions that would be required to keep 2026 total AI capex within the $145 billion upper bound in that failure scenario. The stress-test output, reviewed by the CFO and Chief Strategy Officer, must include explicit trigger thresholds (e.g., pod output KPIs at the 12-week and 26-week marks) whose breach automatically initiates a board-level capital reallocation review, and must be appended to the CADR filed under AC-STR-004-RIF-01. If trigger thresholds cannot be defined because pod KPIs have not yet been set, the Chief People Officer and heads of Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator must submit draft KPIs to the CFO within 30 days of 20 May 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires stress-testing a capex upper bound against pod underperformance; this adaptation names the two specific pods, anchors the four-quarter window to the 20 May 2026 start date, sets a 60-day completion deadline, links the output to the CADR filing, and installs a KPI-definition fallback — converting a high-level stress-test requirement into a sequenced, accountable governance process.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: The workforce-impact assessment must be disaggregated by jurisdiction
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Employment Law team must produce a jurisdiction-disaggregated Workforce Impact Assessment (WIA) covering, at minimum: (a) the United States — WARN Act 60-day advance notice obligations for each affected US site and business unit; (b) the United Kingdom — HR1 collective redundancy notification to the Insolvency Service and TULRCA Section 188 45-day collective consultation obligations where 100 or more employees are affected at a single establishment; and (c) Singapore — compliance with the MOM Tripartite Advisory on Managing Excess Manpower, including the requirement to notify MOM via the Retrenchment Notification portal within five business days of the first notifications issued at 4 a.m. SGT on 20 May 2026. Because employee notifications commenced on 20 May 2026, the WIA must include an explicit retroactive gap analysis confirming whether each jurisdiction's pre-notification filing or consultation-initiation deadline was met; any gap must be escalated to the General Counsel within 48 hours of WIA completion, with a remediation plan filed within five business days.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a jurisdiction-disaggregated workforce impact assessment; this adaptation binds it to Meta's three live notification jurisdictions, names the specific statutory instruments in each (WARN Act, HR1/TULRCA, MOM Tripartite Advisory), anchors the retroactive gap analysis to the confirmed 20 May 2026 4 a.m. SGT notification start, and sets concrete escalation timelines.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Severance and notice obligations for the 8
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Total Rewards and Employment Law teams must jointly produce a Severance Obligation Validation Matrix covering each of the approximately 8,000 employees notified on or after 20 May 2026, disaggregated by the three primary notification jurisdictions: (a) United States — statutory and contractual notice periods, COBRA eligibility windows, and any WARN Act pay-in-lieu obligations; (b) United Kingdom — statutory redundancy pay calculations under the Employment Rights Act 1996, notice entitlements, and any enhanced contractual severance; and (c) Singapore — retrenchment benefits per the Employment Act and applicable collective agreements, and MOM advisory minimums. A documented legal sign-off, countersigned by in-jurisdiction external counsel where Meta lacks licensed local employment counsel, must be obtained for each jurisdiction before final severance calculations are transmitted to payroll for processing. Any discrepancy between the matrix and actual pay calculations must be flagged to the CFO and General Counsel before payment is released.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires validation of severance obligations per jurisdiction; this adaptation names the specific statutory frameworks in each of Meta's three active notification jurisdictions, ties the obligation to the confirmed 8,000-employee population and the 20 May 2026 notification date, and installs a payroll-release gate controlled by documented legal sign-off.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: The reskilling and transition programme for ~7
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's People team, in partnership with the leads of Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator, must within 30 days of 20 May 2026 produce documented competency gap assessments for each of the approximately 7,000 employees being redirected into those pods, mapping each individual's current role profile against the target role requirements; assessments must be reviewed with the affected employee and countersigned by their receiving manager. Each redirected employee must then be placed on a structured transition plan of no fewer than 90 calendar days — commencing from the date of their individual role-change notification — that includes defined learning milestones, assigned mentorship or coaching support, and a mid-point check-in at day 45 documented in Meta's HRIS. The Employment Law team must certify, before any transition plan is finalised, that the new role and its associated pay, grade, and reporting line do not constitute a unilateral material variation of contract that could ground a constructive dismissal claim in the employee's jurisdiction of employment.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires competency gap assessments and a supported transition period; this adaptation ties the obligation to Meta's named Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator pods, sets a 30-day deadline for assessments, specifies the 90-day minimum with a day-45 HRIS checkpoint, and adds a constructive-dismissal legal certification gate before plans are issued.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Employee relations escalation protocols must account for the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Employee Relations and Legal Privacy teams must, within 14 days of 20 May 2026, conduct and document a formal review of whether any data collected under the internally reported 'Model Capability Initiative' (MCI) — including but not limited to mouse-movement logs, keystroke records, or screen snapshots — was accessed, referenced, or used as an input in any selection-for-redundancy decision affecting the approximately 8,000 employees notified on 20 May 2026; this review must produce a written attestation from the CHRO and Chief Privacy Officer confirming whether such use occurred and, if so, identifying the legal basis relied upon in each affected jurisdiction. Pending completion of that review, any further use of MCI-derived data in employment decisions (including decisions affecting the approximately 7,000 employees being redirected into new pods) is suspended unless the Chief Privacy Officer provides written confirmation that a disclosed, jurisdiction-compliant legal basis exists. If the review confirms MCI data was used in redundancy selection without disclosed legal basis, the General Counsel must escalate to the audit committee and initiate a regulatory notification assessment within five business days.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires that employee relations protocols address surveillance data in redundancy selection; this adaptation names the specific 'Model Capability Initiative', ties the review obligation to the confirmed 8,000-person notification event of 20 May 2026, installs a suspension gate on further MCI use pending review, and creates a regulatory escalation trigger if unlawful use is confirmed — all elements absent from the generic control.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Retention risk assessment for the ~7
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's People Analytics team, under the direction of the CHRO, must complete a Retention Risk Assessment (RRA) for all approximately 7,000 employees being redirected into Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator pods no later than 19 June 2026 (30 days post-notification). The RRA must flag, for each individual, whether the new role represents a material change in grade, compensation band, management level, or functional scope relative to their role as of 19 May 2026; individuals assessed as experiencing a material downward change must be categorised as elevated voluntary-attrition risk and elevated employment-tribunal risk, and must be assigned a dedicated HR Business Partner contact within five business days of the RRA being finalised. The Employment Law team must review all elevated-tribunal-risk cases and confirm, before any role-change letter is issued, that the variation has been documented as agreed or that a settlement or enhanced-severance option has been offered where the change cannot be justified.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a 30-day retention risk assessment for redirected employees; this adaptation anchors the 30-day deadline to the confirmed 20 May 2026 notification date, names the two specific destination pods, defines 'material demotion or role-mismatch' in measurable terms (grade, compensation, management level), and installs a legal review gate before role-change letters are issued to high-risk individuals.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: The external communications strategy must reconcile the Janelle
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Corporate Communications and Investor Relations teams must, before issuing any further external press statements or earnings commentary referencing the 20 May 2026 restructuring, produce a Narrative Alignment Review that explicitly maps the language used in Janelle Gale's internal memo (framing the cuts as running 'the company more efficiently') against the public disclosure of $56.31 billion record Q1 2026 quarterly revenue, and identifies any framing inconsistency that a regulator, journalist, or plaintiffs' counsel could characterise as the restructuring being primarily profit-driven rather than efficiency-necessitated. The review must be signed off by the General Counsel and Chief Communications Officer, and must result in a single approved external talking-points document — covering media, analyst calls, and regulatory enquiries — that is distributed to all authorised spokespersons before Q2 2026 earnings communications commence. Any spokesperson who deviates from approved talking points in a material way must report the deviation to Corporate Communications within 24 hours so that Investor Relations can assess whether a corrective disclosure is required.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires reconciling internal and external framing; this adaptation names the specific document (Janelle Gale's memo), the specific financial figure ($56.31B Q1 2026 revenue), the specific narrative tension (efficiency framing versus record profit backdrop), and installs a pre-earnings sign-off gate and a spokesperson deviation reporting obligation.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Stakeholder communications to major institutional shareholders — including
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Investor Relations and Legal teams must treat any communication to institutional shareholders or sell-side analysts that references projected annualised savings from the 20 May 2026 restructuring — including any figure in the range publicly attributed to Bank of America analysts of $7–8 billion — as presumptively material non-public information until that figure is either confirmed or disclaimed in a public filing, and must not disclose it selectively in one-on-one calls, roadshows, or written communications prior to its inclusion in Meta's next 10-Q or an accompanying 8-K filed simultaneously. The Regulation FD compliance officer must review all scheduled analyst and investor engagements between 20 May 2026 and the 10-Q filing date, approve a standardised savings-reference script limited to publicly available information, and document each engagement in the Reg FD log within 24 hours of its occurrence. Any institutional investor communication that references savings projections beyond the approved script must be escalated to the General Counsel within four business hours for assessment of whether a corrective public disclosure is required.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires coordinating shareholder communications with 10-Q filing to prevent selective disclosure; this adaptation identifies the specific unconfirmed $7–8B savings figure as the Reg FD trigger, names the 20 May 2026 restructuring announcement as the materiality anchor, and installs a pre-engagement script approval process with a same-day Reg FD logging requirement.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Innovation governance for the newly formed Applied AI
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Chief Technology Officer and Chief AI Officer must, within 45 days of 20 May 2026, establish and present to the board's technology or audit committee a 12-month milestone framework for the Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator pods, covering at minimum quarterly output targets (Q3 2026, Q4 2026, Q1 2027, Q2 2027) denominated in measurable deliverables — such as model deployment counts, latency benchmarks, or revenue-attributable product integrations — rather than activity metrics. The framework must include explicit kill-switch criteria: pre-defined quantitative thresholds whose breach at any quarterly review automatically triggers a board-level decision on whether to continue, restructure, or wind down the relevant pod, with a corresponding capital reallocation recommendation given that $125–145 billion in 2026 AI capex has been publicly committed to funding these and related initiatives before pod output has been independently validated. The framework must be reviewed by external technical advisers with no prior engagement on Meta's AI capex programme before board submission.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a 12-month milestone framework with kill-switch criteria for new AI pods; this adaptation names the specific pods, anchors the framework to the confirmed $125–145B 2026 capex commitment made before pod validation, sets a 45-day deadline for board presentation, requires output-denominated rather than activity-denominated targets, and mandates independent external review to address the conflict-of-interest risk inherent in internally self-assessed milestones.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Scenario planning for the AI transformation strategy must
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Meta's Chief Operating Officer must commission, within 21 days of 20 May 2026, a concurrent-execution scenario analysis quantifying the operational risk of simultaneously processing approximately 8,000 employee separations and onboarding approximately 7,000 employees into Applied AI Engineering and Agent Transformation Accelerator pods during the same window, with particular attention to: (a) People team and HRIS capacity constraints that could cause notification, severance processing, or transition-plan errors across all three active jurisdictions (US, UK, Singapore); (b) knowledge-transfer and institutional-memory loss if departing employees and receiving pod members overlap in technical domain; and (c) the probability and cost impact of pod ramp-up being delayed beyond 30 September 2026 (end of Q3 2026) due to onboarding bottlenecks. The scenario analysis must include at least two contingency plans — one for a 60-day ramp delay and one for a 120-day ramp delay — each specifying the capex milestone adjustments, additional hiring or contractor triggers, and board escalation steps required to maintain the 2026 AI capex programme within the $145 billion upper bound. The COO must present findings and selected contingency plans to the CEO and CFO before 11 June 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires scenario planning for concurrent separation and pod onboarding; this adaptation defines the precise concurrency tension (8,000 separations versus 7,000 onboardings in the same window), names the three jurisdictions adding execution complexity, sets Q3 2026 as the critical ramp deadline, specifies two concrete delay scenarios with capex linkage, and requires COO ownership with a hard presentation deadline.

incidents.headline: Standard Chartered announces 7,800 AI-driven back-office job cuts
incidents.summary: On 19 May 2026, Standard Chartered CEO Bill Winters disclosed at an investor day in Hong Kong that the bank will eliminate more than 15% of its corporate functions workforce — approximately 7,800 roles out of ~52,000 back-office staff — by 2030. The cuts target human resources, risk, compliance, and finance operations hubs in Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw as the bank scales AI and automation to streamline processes. The disclosure came alongside upgraded shareholder return targets (ROTE above 15% in 2028 rising to ~18% by 2030), and the bank's Hong Kong-listed shares rose 2.5% on the day.

secondary_mappings.why: Elimination of 7,800 back-office roles across multiple geographies over four years requires multi-jurisdiction workforce planning, reskilling programmes, and attrition management.
CEO's public framing of cuts as replacing 'lower-value human capital' generated immediate reputational controversy and sets an industry benchmark pressure on peer banks.
Phased redundancies across India, Malaysia, Poland, and China trigger multi-jurisdictional labour-law notification and consultation obligations.
The restructuring programme will generate material one-time severance and restructuring charges that must be disclosed in StanChart's financial statements and investor guidance.

incidents.severity_rationale: Affects 15% of a major global bank's support workforce across four years with multi-geography execution risk; magnitude is high but phased timeline and strong financial backdrop (record operating income of $5.9B) reduce immediate operational severity.

adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires governance linkage to ROTE milestones; this adaptation anchors those milestones to the specific percentages and years disclosed at the 19 May 2026 investor day and ties the attestation obligation to the four named GBS sites where FCA-regulated compliance and risk functions will actually be stood down.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Conduct a scenario stress-test by Q3 2026 modelling
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Chief Risk Officer must commission and deliver to the Board Risk Committee by 30 September 2026 a quantified stress-test modelling two scenarios: (a) a 12-month delay and (b) a 24-month delay in AI automation go-live across the compliance and finance-ops functions in Bengaluru and Kuala Lumpur specifically, with each scenario calculating the resulting headcount cost over-run, the projected ROTE shortfall against the >15% 2028 and ~18% 2030 targets disclosed at the 19 May 2026 investor day, and the residual control risk from retained but under-resourced human teams during the delay window. The stress-test must include a remediation action plan triggered if either scenario materialises, and the completed report with Board Risk Committee sign-off constitutes the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control calls for scenario stress-testing; this adaptation specifies the exact delay intervals, the two highest-risk hub locations for compliance automation, the precise ROTE guidance figures against which shortfall must be measured, and the public commitment date that creates investor-expectation risk if the programme slips.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish a board-level AI transformation steering committee with
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered must establish, by 31 July 2026, a board-level AI Transformation Steering Committee chaired by a non-executive director and including the CRO, CCO, CFO, and the Group Head of People, with a formal charter requiring committee approval before each automation go-live wave displaces incumbent roles at any of the four named GBS hubs (Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, Warsaw). The charter must specify that no role within FCA-regulated compliance or risk functions at those sites may be stood down until the committee has confirmed in writing that the replacing AI or automated process has passed a minimum 90-day parallel-run validation period with zero material control failures. The signed committee charter, meeting minutes, and each wave's sign-off record constitute the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a steering committee with displacement accountability; this adaptation defines the mandatory parallel-run validation gate, fixes the 90-day threshold, restricts the stand-down prohibition specifically to FCA-regulated functions, and names all four GBS sites as the operative scope of the approval obligation.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Produce a jurisdiction-disaggregated workforce-impact assessment covering India (Industrial
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of People must, before any formal notice of redundancy is issued to any of the approximately 7,800 affected employees, deliver to Group ExCo a jurisdiction-disaggregated workforce-impact assessment that addresses: for India (Bengaluru and Chennai), whether aggregate redundancies trigger the 100-employee threshold under the Industrial Disputes Act 1947 requiring government permission; for Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), compliance with Employment Act 1955 collective-dismissal notification obligations to the Director General of Labour; for Poland (Warsaw), compliance with Labour Code Chapter XI requirements for employer notification to the District Labour Office and works-council consultation; and for any UK-domiciled corporate-function roles, compliance with TULRCA 1992 45-day collective consultation requirements. Each jurisdiction section must be signed off by locally qualified employment counsel, and the completed assessment must be filed with the Group Legal function at least 30 days prior to the earliest planned notice date.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a multi-jurisdiction workforce-impact assessment; this adaptation names the specific statutory thresholds and competent authorities in each of the four hub countries and adds the UK as a potential additional jurisdiction, requiring legal sign-off by country-qualified counsel rather than a generic HR review.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Issue a formal employee-communication and consultation plan for the 7
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of People, accountable to CEO Bill Winters, must issue a formal employee-communication and consultation plan by 18 June 2026 (within 30 days of the 19 May 2026 investor-day disclosure) that specifies, for each of the four GBS hubs, the expected role-elimination timeline by function (HR, risk, compliance, finance-ops), the minimum consultation period before any formal notice is issued, and the redeployment or reskilling pathways available at that site. The plan must be communicated directly to all employees in scope at Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw simultaneously to prevent information asymmetry across jurisdictions, and a copy must be provided to employee representative bodies in Malaysia and Poland in accordance with local statutory requirements. Evidence of completion is the dated communication pack, distribution records for each site, and confirmation of delivery to representative bodies.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a consultation plan within 30 days; this adaptation anchors the deadline to the specific 19 May 2026 disclosure date, requires simultaneous multi-site communication to prevent jurisdictional information asymmetry, and integrates the mandatory delivery to Malaysian and Polish representative bodies within the same 30-day window.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Define and fund a reskilling programme for at-risk HR
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered must define and fund a reskilling programme, with a named Programme Director reporting directly to the Group Head of People, covering HR, risk, compliance, and finance-ops staff at risk across Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw, and publish the programme's curriculum, funding envelope, and site-level enrolment targets to the Board by 31 December 2026. The programme must set measurable enrolment targets of no less than 40% of the identified at-risk population at each GBS hub by end-2027, with the Programme Director providing a half-yearly progress report to the CEO tied explicitly to the workforce transition commitment made at the 19 May 2026 investor day. Evidence of completion is the board-approved programme document, the published funding commitment, and the first half-yearly progress report.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a funded reskilling programme with measurable targets; this adaptation sets a specific minimum enrolment threshold of 40% per hub, links the Programme Director's reporting line directly to the CEO's public investor-day commitment, and establishes the 31 December 2026 programme-definition deadline as the accountability date.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement a real-time headcount-tracking mechanism across the Bengaluru
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of People must implement, by 31 August 2026, a real-time headcount-tracking dashboard covering the Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw GBS hubs that records, by function and by month, actual automation-driven role eliminations against the phase-gate schedule underpinning the 7,800-role target, and automatically flags to Group Legal and the local HR Director any hub where cumulative departures within a rolling 90-day window are approaching the statutory threshold that triggers mandatory collective-dismissal consultation obligations in that jurisdiction. The dashboard must generate a monthly exception report to the AI Transformation Steering Committee, and the system go-live date, configuration documentation, and first monthly exception report constitute the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a headcount-tracking mechanism; this adaptation specifies the 90-day rolling window trigger aligned to collective-dismissal consultation thresholds in the relevant jurisdictions, requires automatic alerting to Group Legal (not just HR), and ties the reporting cadence to the AI Transformation Steering Committee established under MC-STR-020.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Assess and document the impact of the AI
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of People and Group D&I Lead must jointly produce, by 30 November 2026, a documented diversity-impact assessment of the AI automation programme's effect on the affected corporate-function workforce at all four GBS sites, disaggregated by gender, local-national status, and grade band, with specific analysis of whether the programme disproportionately displaces women and local-national employees concentrated in back-office roles at the Bengaluru and Chennai hubs. Where the assessment identifies a statistically disproportionate impact on any protected group at any site, it must propose concrete mitigation measures — such as adjusted reskilling prioritisation or phased stand-down sequencing — for Board People Committee approval before the relevant automation wave proceeds. The completed assessment, its statistical annexes, and any Board People Committee decisions constitute the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a D&I impact assessment; this adaptation directs the analysis specifically at the Bengaluru and Chennai concentration risk for women and local-national staff noted in the reported facts, requires statistical disaggregation by grade band, and mandates Board People Committee approval of mitigations before an automation wave can proceed where disproportionate impact is found.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Develop a coordinated stakeholder-communications protocol — covering institutional investors
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of Corporate Affairs, supported by Group Legal and the Chief People Officer, must deliver by 2 June 2026 (within 14 days of the 19 May 2026 investor-day announcement) a coordinated stakeholder-communications protocol establishing a single approved narrative on the 7,800-role displacement programme and assigning named spokespersons for: institutional investors and sell-side analysts, the FCA, employee representative bodies in Malaysia (National Union of Bank Employees) and Poland (works council at the Warsaw hub), and financial media in India, Malaysia, and Poland. The protocol must specify escalation procedures for off-message communications, mandate that any regulator-facing or media-facing statement referencing the automation programme is reviewed by Group Legal before release, and be filed with the Board Audit Committee. The dated protocol document and the filing confirmation constitute the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a coordinated communications protocol; this adaptation names the specific Malaysian trade union and the Polish works council as mandatory addressees, fixes the 14-day deadline to the precise investor-day date, and adds a pre-release legal-review gate for all regulator-facing and media-facing statements to manage the multi-jurisdictional regulatory exposure.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Monitor and respond to adverse media and social-media
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's Group Head of Corporate Affairs must activate, within five business days of the 19 May 2026 disclosure, a 24/7 media and social-media monitoring brief covering English, Bahasa Malaysia, Hindi, Tamil, and Polish language sources, tracking sentiment and coverage of the 7,800-role cut across India (Economic Times, Business Standard, The Hindu BusinessLine), Malaysia (The Edge, Free Malaysia Today), and Poland (Puls Biznesu, Rzeczpospolita), with daily sentiment reports delivered to the CEO's office and Group External Affairs through at least 30 June 2026. Any article or post attributing union or government criticism to Standard Chartered's AI automation strategy must trigger a same-day escalation to the Group Head of Corporate Affairs and the communications protocol owner for a coordinated response decision within four business hours. Evidence of completion is the monitoring activation record, the daily report log, and the escalation response records.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires adverse-media monitoring; this adaptation specifies the exact language markets and named media outlets in each of the three highest-risk jurisdictions, sets a five-business-day activation deadline anchored to the 19 May 2026 disclosure, and prescribes a four-business-hour response SLA for union- or government-critical coverage.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Disclose in the 2026 interim financial results the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's CFO must ensure that the 2026 interim financial results (expected H1 2026 report) include a separately presented restructuring charge line item attributable exclusively to the approximately 7,800-role AI automation programme announced on 19 May 2026, quantifying both the total expected restructuring charge and the projected annualised cost savings, and explicitly distinguishing these from any prior-period restructuring provisions recognised before 19 May 2026. The disclosure must comply with IFRS 3 recognition criteria and FCA DTR 4.2 transparency requirements, reference the investor-day ROTE upgrade to >15% by 2028 and ~18% by 2030 as the strategic context, and be reviewed by the external auditor before publication. The evidence of completion is the published interim results document, the auditor's review confirmation, and the CFO's sign-off memorandum to the Audit Committee.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires disclosure of restructuring charges in interim results; this adaptation requires explicit segregation from pre-19 May 2026 provisions to prevent blending with prior restructuring activity, ties the disclosure narrative directly to the ROTE targets that created investor expectations, and names the external auditor review as a mandatory pre-publication step.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Validate that the capex investment in AI and
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's CFO and Group Controller must, by the date of the 2026 Annual Report publication, prepare and Audit Committee-approve a documented accounting policy paper setting out, for each category of AI and automation tooling investment funding the 7,800-role reduction, whether it is capitalised as an intangible asset under IAS 38 (with the capitalisation criteria, amortisation period, and impairment trigger disclosed) or expensed as incurred, and must disclose this treatment and the aggregate capitalised and expensed amounts in the 2026 Annual Report notes to the financial statements. The paper must address specifically the treatment of implementation costs for third-party AI platforms deployed at the Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw hubs, given the materiality of these investments to the ROTE improvement trajectory committed at the 19 May 2026 investor day. The Audit Committee-approved policy paper and the relevant Annual Report disclosure notes constitute the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires IAS 38 accounting treatment validation; this adaptation requires the policy paper to address third-party AI platform implementation costs at each of the four named GBS hubs specifically, links the materiality judgement to the ROTE improvement trajectory disclosed publicly at the investor day, and requires Audit Committee approval before the Annual Report is filed.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish and disclose a benefits-realisation tracking mechanism that
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Standard Chartered's CFO must establish, by 30 September 2026, a benefits-realisation tracking mechanism that reconciles, at each half-year close (H1 and H2) from 2026 through 2030, actual headcount cost savings achieved from the AI automation programme against the savings trajectory required to deliver the ROTE targets of >15% by 2028 and ~18% by 2030 committed at the 19 May 2026 investor day, with the variance analysis and updated forecast presented to the Audit Committee within 30 days of each half-year close. The mechanism must track savings by GBS hub (Bengaluru, Chennai, Kuala Lumpur, Warsaw) and by function (HR, risk, compliance, finance-ops) so that slippage in any specific hub or function can be isolated and remediated before it compounds into a material ROTE shortfall. Evidence of completion is the documented methodology approved by the Audit Committee, the first half-year reconciliation report, and the Audit Committee meeting minutes confirming receipt.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a benefits-realisation mechanism reported to the Audit Committee; this adaptation requires hub- and function-level disaggregation of savings so that localised automation slippage is detectable before it becomes a material ROTE miss, and fixes the 30-day post-close reporting SLA to the specific half-year reporting cycle rather than an undefined periodic cadence.

incidents.headline: Inter IKEA Group cuts 850 jobs amid consumer spending fall
incidents.summary: On 18 May 2026, Inter IKEA Group — the IKEA franchisor and brand owner — announced it would slash 850 jobs in a cost-cutting drive as weakening consumer spending weighs on the global retail furniture market. The reduction targets corporate and support functions and is part of a broader effort to reduce operating costs as the Group responds to a structural slowdown in discretionary consumer expenditure across its key markets in Europe and North America. The move signals a strategic contraction at the operational core of the world's largest furniture retailer amid deteriorating demand conditions.

secondary_mappings.why: 850 job eliminations at a non-listed franchisor require workforce transition management and could trigger consultation obligations in European jurisdictions.
IKEA's brand identity is tied to affordability and worker welfare; visible cost-cutting during a consumer downturn risks brand perception damage with its core value-conscious customer base.

The cost-cutting drive indicates revenue pressure sufficient to require operating-expense reduction, signalling potential margin stress at the franchisor level that controls IKEA's global IP and supply arrangements.

incidents.severity_rationale: 850 jobs at a privately-held global franchisor is notable as a demand-signal from one of the world's most recognised retail brands, but the absolute headcount impact is modest and the timeline is not disclosed as immediate.

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure that strategic restructuring decisions at Inter IKEA Group are grounded in documented capability-impact analysis, preventing inadvertent destruction of core product-design and franchise-support competencies in the Almhult and Delft hubs.
Ensure Inter IKEA Group's 850-role reduction is executed with lawful, fair, and transparent workforce-management processes across all affected jurisdictions, including Sweden and the Netherlands.
Ensure Inter IKEA Group maintains continuity of institutional knowledge and critical capability in range-design and franchise-operations functions during and after the 2026 restructuring programme.
Protect Inter IKEA Group's brand reputation and stakeholder trust during a publicly disclosed 850-job restructuring that coincides with a second consecutive year of declining IKEA sales.
Ensure internal and external communications regarding the 2026 restructuring are consistent, accurate, and pre-empt misinformation across Inter IKEA's 63-country franchise network.
Ensure Inter IKEA Group accurately recognises, discloses, and tracks restructuring charges arising from the May 2026 850-headcount reduction, including severance provisions and any asset impairments.
Ensure that cost savings realised from the 850-role reduction are demonstrably re-invested into IKEA's stated affordability and price-reduction strategy rather than absorbed by other cost growth.

peer_watchlist.name: Ingka Group
peer_watchlist.exposure_reason: Ingka already cut ~800 roles in March 2026; Inter IKEA's simultaneous support-function reduction directly constrains Ingka's product-ranging pipeline from the shared Almhult design hub.

source_anchor.citation: Ingka Group ~800-role cut announced ~2 months before Inter IKEA's 850 cut, Retail Dive, 18 May 2026

peer_watchlist.name: JYSK A/S
peer_watchlist.exposure_reason: JYSK is IKEA's closest European value-furniture competitor across 52 countries; Inter IKEA's explicit price-reduction strategy post-restructuring directly threatens JYSK's margin positioning.

peer_watchlist.name: Steinhoff International Holdings N.V. (Pepco/Poundland parent)

graded_claims.claim: Inter IKEA Group announced it would cut 850 jobs globally.
Approximately 300 of the 850 affected roles are in Sweden, at Inter IKEA's Almhult hub.
The 850 cuts represent approximately 3% of Inter IKEA's 27,500-strong global workforce.
CFO Henrik Elm stated the company had grown 'too complex and too fragmented' and that cuts target support and corporate functions.
The restructuring is planned to be completed by end of 2026.
Inter IKEA reported a 32% drop in net profit, to €1.5 billion, in fiscal year 2024/25 ending August 2025.
Inter IKEA's largest franchisee Ingka Group also announced approximately 800 job cuts roughly two months prior.
Rising U.S. tariffs have materially pressured Inter IKEA's cost base.
Consumer confidence has been impacted by the Iran war, with higher fuel prices reducing discretionary spending.
Inter IKEA and Best Buy are in an active shop-in-shop pilot, with Best Buy now opening consultation spaces inside two IKEA stores.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must document a capability-retention map
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief Product Officer must commission and deliver a capability-retention map for the Almhult product-design hub no later than 14 days before the first Swedish redundancy selection letter is issued, cataloguing each of the approximately 300 at-risk Almhult roles against their contribution to the FY2026/27 range development calendar and flagging any role where vacancy would delay a committed product milestone. The map must be reviewed and signed off by the CEO Jakub Jankowski and must be used as a binding input to the selection criteria applied by Almhult HR, with any role rated 'critical-path' requiring documented executive sign-off before inclusion in the redundancy pool. Completion is evidenced by the dated, signed capability map lodged with Group Legal and a written confirmation from the Chief Product Officer that the FY2026/27 calendar has been cross-referenced against final selections.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a capability-retention map in the abstract; this adaptation ties it to the specific Almhult hub, the confirmed 300-role Swedish tranche, the FY2026/27 product calendar as the concrete asset at risk, and the pre-selection deadline that gives the map operational force rather than retrospective documentary value.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group's new CEO Jakub Jankowski (appointed
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group CEO Jakub Jankowski must formally approve a post-restructuring operating model document by 30 September 2026 — ahead of the confirmed end-2026 restructuring completion date — that explicitly maps how the reduced corporate and support functions will continue to deliver Inter IKEA's franchisor obligations (brand stewardship, range development, supply standards, and format governance) to all 13 franchisees operating across 63 countries. The document must address the CFO's stated driver that the organisation had become 'too complex and too fragmented' by articulating which eliminated functions will be absorbed, automated, or redistributed, and must be shared with all 13 franchisee leadership teams with a Q4 2026 briefing call on record. Evidence of completion is the CEO's dated signature on the operating model document, the franchisee distribution list with delivery receipts, and the briefing call minutes.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires approval of a post-restructuring model; this adaptation names the approver (Jankowski), anchors the rationale in the CFO's confirmed 'too complex and too fragmented' diagnosis, specifies the franchisee network dimensions (13 franchisees, 63 countries), and sets a deadline that precedes the confirmed end-2026 completion date so the model governs execution rather than ratifying a fait accompli.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must disaggregate the 850 redundancies
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Group Legal and HR functions must, within 10 business days of the 18 May 2026 announcement, produce a jurisdiction-disaggregated redundancy matrix that separately analyses the approximately 300 Almhult (Sweden) roles against Swedish LAS collective-notice requirements — including the mandatory 30-day advance notification to the Swedish Public Employment Service (Arbetsförmedlingen) for redundancies exceeding 25 employees — and the approximately 550 Delft (Netherlands) roles against WMCO collective-consultation obligations, including the requirement to notify the UWV and relevant trade unions before implementation. The matrix must confirm whether each jurisdiction's notification threshold is met, identify the filing deadlines, and be submitted to the relevant authorities with copies retained in the Group Legal compliance register. Evidence of completion is the dated authority-filing receipts for both jurisdictions and the signed-off matrix held by the Chief People Officer.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires jurisdiction testing; this adaptation names both specific statutory regimes (Swedish LAS and Dutch WMCO), the specific authority bodies (Arbetsförmedlingen and UWV), the confirmed role counts by location, and the 10-business-day window that makes compliance actionable before consultation periods expire.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must initiate formal Works Council
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief People Officer must initiate formal Works Council (Ondernemingsraad, OR) consultation at Inter IKEA's Delft HQ under Dutch Works Councils Act (WOR) Article 25 no later than 25 May 2026 — within 7 days of the 18 May announcement — covering the approximately 550 non-Sweden redundancies, and must ensure the OR receives a written advice request that details the business rationale (including the CFO's stated complexity-reduction objective and the confirmed 32% net-profit decline as reported context), the proposed selection criteria, and the social plan. No redundancy notices may be issued to Delft-based employees until the OR has issued its formal advice and the mandatory 30-day implementation suspension period has elapsed or been waived in writing by the OR. The consultation record, OR advice document, and any OR response period waiver must be retained in the Group Legal compliance register as evidence of procedural compliance.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires Works Council consultation; this adaptation specifies the Delft jurisdiction, the confirmed ~550-role scope, the WOR Article 25 trigger, a hard initiation deadline of 7 days post-announcement, and the prohibition on issuing notices before advice is received — converting a procedural requirement into an operational gate.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must publish individual career-transition plans
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief People Officer must ensure that each of the 850 affected employees receives an individualised career-transition plan — covering internal redeployment options, outplacement support, skills assessment, and applicable severance entitlements — no later than the date on which their role-elimination notice takes formal legal effect under the applicable jurisdiction's notice period. The transition plans must be delivered through a dedicated HR case manager assigned to each affected employee, consistent with the process publicly committed to by Inter IKEA's Portuguese country manager in May 2026, and must be documented in the Group's HR case-management system with a completion timestamp per employee. Evidence of completion is an aggregate delivery report from the HR case-management system confirming 100% of notified employees received their plan on or before their role-elimination date, reviewed and signed off by the Chief People Officer.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires transition plans; this adaptation ties the delivery deadline to each employee's individual role-elimination date (not a single group deadline), references the specific public commitment made by the Portuguese country manager as the accountability anchor, and specifies the HR case-management system as the evidencing mechanism across the full 850-person cohort.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must assess the risk of
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief People Officer must commission an independent demographic-impact assessment of the 850-role redundancy cohort before any selection decisions are finalised, specifically testing for disproportionate adverse impact on protected characteristics — with particular attention to age and tenure concentration in Almhult's product-heritage and design functions, where long-service employees are confirmed to be concentrated among the approximately 300 Swedish roles. The assessment must apply the 80% (four-fifths) rule or equivalent statistical test across age bands, gender, and nationality within both the Almhult and Delft cohorts separately, and must be reviewed by an external employment-law counsel before selections are communicated. Any characteristic where the selection rate falls below the 80% threshold must trigger a documented remediation review before that tranche proceeds, with findings and remediation records retained in the Group Legal compliance register. Evidence of completion is the dated assessment report, external counsel sign-off, and the remediation log.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a demographic-impact assessment; this adaptation names the specific risk vector (long-tenure product-heritage staff concentration at Almhult), applies a recognised statistical test standard, separates the two primary geographic cohorts, and creates a hard gate — the 80% threshold — that blocks a tranche from proceeding rather than producing a post-hoc report.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must establish a retained-knowledge transfer
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief Product Officer must implement a structured knowledge-transfer protocol for every Almhult role rated 'critical-path' in the capability-retention map (AC-STR-021-RIF-01) before that role is vacated, requiring each departing employee to complete a minimum four-week documented knowledge-transfer period covering: range-design rationale for in-development products, supplier relationship context, and prototype-stage institutional decisions not captured in formal project documentation. Each transfer must produce a role-specific knowledge record stored in Inter IKEA's product-development knowledge repository (or an interim equivalent if no such system currently exists), reviewed and accepted in writing by the relevant product-development team lead. No critical-path Almhult role may be formally vacated until the relevant team lead has signed acceptance of the knowledge record. Evidence of completion is the set of signed acceptance records per critical-path role, held by the Chief Product Officer.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a knowledge-transfer protocol; this adaptation anchors it to the confirmed Almhult 300-role tranche, ties it operationally to the capability map produced under AC-STR-021-RIF-01, specifies the four-week minimum transfer period, names the specific knowledge categories at risk (range-design rationale, supplier context, prototype decisions), and creates a role-vacation gate conditional on team-lead acceptance.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must issue a single
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief Communications Officer, acting under authority of CEO Jakub Jankowski, must distribute a single authoritative franchisee communication to the leadership of all 13 Inter IKEA franchisees no later than 23 May 2026 (5 calendar days after the 18 May announcement), explicitly confirming that: (a) the 850 roles cut are exclusively in corporate and support functions as stated by CFO Henrik Elm; (b) product supply chains, range development timelines, and format standards for all 63 franchise countries are unaffected; and (c) Inter IKEA's franchisor obligations remain fully operational. The communication must be a single controlled document — not an informal briefing — signed by the CEO, and must be accompanied by a Q&A annex addressing anticipated franchisee questions about continuity. Delivery receipts or read-confirmations from all 13 franchisee contacts must be logged by the Communications team. Evidence of completion is the dated document, the full delivery receipt log, and the CEO's signature.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a franchisee communication; this adaptation fixes the 5-day deadline to a hard calendar date (23 May 2026), names the specific sender authority (CEO Jankowski), specifies the three content obligations tied to confirmed facts (CFO's statement, supply continuity, franchisor obligations), and requires individual delivery receipts from all 13 franchisees rather than a broadcast with no confirmation.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group's CFO Henrik Elm must maintain
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group CFO Henrik Elm must, before any media appearance or investor briefing following the 18 May 2026 Reuters interview, receive pre-clearance on all quantitative and forward-looking statements from Group Legal and the Chief Communications Officer, using a standardised messaging brief that locks the approved narrative to three confirmed data points: the 850-role figure, the approximately 3% workforce proportion, and the end-2026 completion timeline. Any deviation from these anchors — including discussion of the reported 32% net profit decline, restructuring cost quantum, or tariff pressures — requires explicit pre-approval by the Chief Communications Officer and must be logged in a disclosure register. The CFO must not make new material disclosures about the restructuring outside this pre-clearance framework at any media appearance through to the FY2025/26 annual results. Evidence of compliance is the pre-clearance log with timestamps and approver signatures for each appearance.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires consistent messaging; this adaptation names the specific individual (CFO Elm), anchors the locked narrative to the three confirmed public data points from the 18 May Reuters interview, identifies the specific disclosure risks (profit decline, cost quantum, tariff pressures) that require escalated clearance, and creates a logged pre-clearance gate rather than a general consistency aspiration.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must monitor social media and
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Chief Communications Officer must, from 18 May 2026 through to the confirmed end-2026 restructuring completion, operate a daily social-media and franchise-partner communications monitoring protocol specifically configured to detect conflation of Inter IKEA's 850-role cut with Ingka Group's separate approximately 800-role reduction announced approximately two months prior — given that external audiences and media may aggregate the two announcements into a single, larger workforce reduction narrative. Where conflation, material misstatement of scope, or inaccurate attribution of Ingka Group actions to Inter IKEA is detected, a correction must be issued through the same channel within 24 hours, using approved language pre-cleared by Group Legal. A monitoring log recording all detected instances and correction actions must be maintained and reviewed weekly by the Chief Communications Officer. Evidence of compliance is the weekly monitoring log and the record of any corrections issued.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires misinformation monitoring; this adaptation identifies the specific and concrete conflation risk (Inter IKEA 850 vs. Ingka Group 800, a reported fact establishing two distinct but proximate announcements), sets a 24-hour correction turnaround, and requires a channel-matched correction response rather than a generic denial.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must quantify and book the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's CFO Henrik Elm must ensure that a full restructuring provision covering the 850-role reduction — itemised across severance pay (by jurisdiction, with Swedish LAS and Dutch WMCO entitlements calculated separately), outplacement and career-transition programme costs, and any facility or lease costs associated with vacated Almhult and Delft space — is recognised in the FY2025/26 accounts ending August 2026, in accordance with IAS 37 constructive-obligation criteria, given that the public announcement on 18 May 2026 constitutes a valid constructive obligation trigger. The provision calculation must be reviewed by Inter IKEA's external auditors before the accounts are finalised, with a specific auditor sign-off on the IAS 37 trigger date and completeness of cost categories. Evidence of completion is the auditor-reviewed provision workbook, the dated IAS 37 trigger-date memorandum, and the provision line in the FY2025/26 accounts.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires booking a restructuring provision; this adaptation ties the IAS 37 constructive-obligation trigger to the specific 18 May 2026 public announcement, requires jurisdiction-separated severance calculations reflecting the two confirmed primary locations (Almhult and Delft), and mandates external auditor sign-off on the trigger date — the point most likely to be disputed — rather than treating the provision as a routine estimate.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group must disclose the projected annualised
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's CFO Henrik Elm must include in the FY2025/26 annual report (to be published following the August 2026 year-end) a dedicated restructuring-savings disclosure that quantifies: (a) the projected annualised cost saving attributable to the 850-role reduction in corporate and support functions; (b) the expected timeline for full realisation, broken down between first-year partial savings (reflecting the end-2026 completion date) and steady-state run-rate; and (c) the mechanism by which cost savings are intended to support lower consumer prices, as explicitly linked by the CFO in public statements. The disclosure must be consistent with the restructuring provision booked under AC-FIN-003-RIF-01 and must be reviewed and approved by the Audit Committee before publication. Evidence of completion is the Audit Committee approval minute referencing the disclosure, and the published annual report containing the itemised disclosure.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires disclosure of projected savings; this adaptation holds the CFO accountable to his own confirmed public linkage between headcount savings and consumer price reduction, requires phasing the savings disclosure to reflect the confirmed end-2026 completion timeline, and cross-references the provision booked under AC-FIN-003-RIF-01 to ensure the savings and cost disclosures are internally consistent.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Inter IKEA Group's treasury must stress-test operating cash
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Inter IKEA Group's Group Treasurer, reporting to CFO Henrik Elm, must complete a cash-flow stress test by 31 July 2026 — before the FY2025/26 accounts are finalised — modelling a scenario in which the reported 32% net profit decline to €1.5 billion in FY2024/25 deepens by a further 15% in FY2025/26, with the full restructuring provision for the 850-role reduction (as calculated under AC-FIN-003-RIF-01) treated as a first-year cash outflow in FY2025/26. The stress test must also incorporate, as a separate sensitivity layer, the reported material cost-base pressure from rising U.S. tariffs, testing whether Inter IKEA's liquidity headroom remains adequate to fund franchise-support obligations and ongoing range-development investment under the combined stress scenario. The output must be a written treasury report with explicit go/no-go liquidity conclusions presented to the CFO and Audit Committee. Evidence of completion is the dated treasury stress-test report and the Audit Committee presentation record.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a stress test; this adaptation anchors the base-case shock to the reported confirmed figures (32% profit decline, €1.5 billion), specifies the incremental 15% deepening as the stress scenario, treats the restructuring provision as a first-year cash outflow (not a non-cash charge), and adds the reported U.S. tariff pressure as a distinct sensitivity layer — producing a stress architecture grounded entirely in the incident's confirmed and reported facts.
